content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob play a game on a pile of NNN stones.

In her turn, Alice can remove **at least** one and **at most** AAA stones while Bob can remove **at least** one and **at most** BBB stones on his turn.

The person who takes the last stone wins.

Predict the winner of the game if Alice goes first (and turns alternate) under optimal play from both players.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers, NNN, AAA and BBB, the number of stones, and the maximum number of stones Alice and Bob can remove in one move respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line `Alice` if Alice wins, or `Bob` if Bob wins.


You may print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `BOB`, `bob`, `Bob`, and `bOB` are all considered identical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, Alice can first remove a single stone from the pile bringing it to 222 stones. Then Bob is forced to remove 111 stone, and Alice is able to take the last stone.
In the second test case, Alice can take all 555 stones on her first move.
In the third test case, the entire game is forced (both players can only remove one stone) and after 10910^9109 turns, Bob takes the last stone and wins.
